segments.text: listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Why are legends handed down by storytellers useful?

translations.zh-Hans: 故事讲述者代代相传的传说为什么有用？

segments.text: We can read of things that happened 5,000 years ago in the Near East, where people first learn to write.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们可以读到五千年前近东发生的事情，那里的人最早学会了书写。

segments.text: But there are some parts of the world where even now people cannot write.

translations.zh-Hans: 但世界上有些地方，即使现在人们仍不会书写。

segments.text: The only way that they can preserve their history is to recount it as sagas.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们保存历史的唯一方式，是把它作为英雄传说讲述下来。

segments.text: Legends handed down from one generation of storytellers to another.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些传说由一代代故事讲述者口耳相传。

segments.text: These legends are useful because they can tell us something about migrations of people who lived long ago.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些传说很有用，因为它们能告诉我们一些远古民族迁徙的情况。

segments.text: But none could write down what they did.

translations.zh-Hans: 但当时没有人能把自己的所作所为记录下来。

segments.text: Anthropologists wondered where the remote ancestors of the Polynesian peoples now living in the Pacific Islands came from.

translations.zh-Hans: 人类学家曾想知道，如今生活在太平洋岛屿上的波利尼西亚人的远祖来自哪里。

segments.text: The sagas of these people explained that some of them came from Indonesia about two thousand years ago.

translations.zh-Hans: 这些民族的传说说明，其中一些人约在两千年前来自印度尼西亚。

segments.text: But the first people who were like ourselves lived so long ago that even their sagas, if they had any, are forgotten.

translations.zh-Hans: 然而，最早与我们相似的人生活的年代太过久远，以至他们的传说——如果曾有的话——也早已被遗忘。

segments.text: So archaeologists have neither history nor legends to help them to find out where the first modern men came from.

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，考古学家既无历史记载也无传说可借助，来查明最早的现代人来自哪里。

segments.text: Fortunately, however, ancient men made tools of stone, especially flint, because this is easier to shape than other kinds.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过幸运的是，古人用石头，特别是比其他石材更容易加工的燧石制作工具。

segments.text: They may also have used wood and skins, but these have rotted away.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们或许也使用过木头和兽皮，但这些早已腐烂。

segments.text: Stone does not decay, and so the tools of long ago have remained when even the bones of the men who made them have disappeared without trace.

translations.zh-Hans: 石头不会腐朽，所以远古的工具保存至今，而制造它们的人的骨骼甚至都已消失得无影无踪。
